inlineContent.text: is a special node that displays what is visible from its current location. Cameras register themselves in the nearest

node (when ascending the tree). Only one camera can be active per viewport. If no viewport is available ascending the tree, the camera will register in the global viewport. In other words, a camera just provides 3D display capabilities to a
, and, without one, a scene registered in that

The horizontal (X) offset of the camera viewport.
The camera’s field of view angle (in degrees). Only applicable in perspective mode. Since
locks one axis,

Most basic 3D game object, parent of all 3D-related nodes.
The vertical (Y) offset of the camera viewport.
The culling mask that describes which
are rendered by this camera. By default, all 20 user-visible layers are rendered.
Server for anything visible.
Parent class for camera settings.

Camera node, displays from a point of view.
Returns a normal vector from the screen point location directed along the camera. Orthogonal cameras are normalized. Perspective cameras account for perspective, screen width/height, etc.
Sets the camera projection to perspective mode (see .projectionPerspective), by specifying a
(field of view) angle in degrees, and the

Returns

if the given position is inside the camera’s frustum (the green part of the linked diagram). [url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/godotengine/godot-docs/master/img/camera3d_position_frustum.png]See this diagram[/url] for an overview of position query methods.

The distance to the near culling boundary for this camera relative to its local Z axis. Lower values allow the camera to see objects more up close to its origin, at the cost of lower precision across the
range. Values lower than the default can lead to increased Z-fighting.
Types that conform to VariantRepresentable can be stored directly in
with no conversion.  These include all of the Variant types from Godot (for
example
), Godot objects (those that subclass
SwiftGodot.Object) as well as the built-in Swift types UInt8, Int64 and Double.
Returns a 3D position in world space, that is the result of projecting a point on the
rectangle by the inverse camera projection. This is useful for casting rays in the form of (origin, normal) for object intersection or picking.
Returns

if the given position is behind the camera (the blue part of the linked diagram). [url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/godotengine/godot-docs/master/img/camera3d_position_frustum.png]See this diagram[/url] for an overview of position query methods.
Types that conform to VariantStorable can be stored in a Variant and can be extracted
back out of a Variant.
Returns the RID of a pyramid shape encompassing the camera’s view frustum, ignoring the camera’s near plane. The tip of the pyramid represents the position of the camera.
Returns the 2D coordinate in the
rectangle that maps to the given 3D point in world space.

The camera’s frustum offset. This can be changed from the default to create “tilted frustum” effects such as [url=https://zdoom.org/wiki/Y-shearing]Y-shearing[/url].
Returns the 3D point in world space that maps to the given 2D coordinate in the
rectangle on a plane that is the given
distance into the scene away from the camera.
Framework to write Godot Game Extensions using the Swift Programming Language.
Returns the projection matrix that this camera uses to render to its associated viewport. The camera must be part of the scene tree to function.
Resource for environment nodes (like

to use for this camera.

rectangle by the inverse camera projection. This is useful for casting rays in the form of (origin, normal) for object intersection or picking.

Abstract base class for viewports. Encapsulates drawing and interaction with a game world.

The camera’s projection mode. In .projectionPerspective mode, objects’ Z distance from the camera’s local space scales their perceived size.
Variant objects box various Godot Objects, you create them with one of the
constructors, and you can retrieve the contents using the various extension
constructors that are declared on the various types that are wrapped.

The camera’s size in meters measured as the diameter of the width or height, depending on

A camera node with a few overrules for AR/VR applied, such as location tracking.
The distance to the far culling boundary for this camera relative to its local Z axis. Higher values allow the camera to see further away, while decreasing
can improve performance if it results in objects being partially or fully culled.
A plane in Hessian normal form.
